title: Reverse Proxies, Forwarded Trust, Message Framing, and Smuggling Defense · Language Lens: Proxy Hop Origin Model
summary: A gateway preserves client Forwarded fields, uses deprecated ReverseProxy.Director, disagrees with an upstream about malformed framing, forwards hop-by-hop fields, and constructs unsafe backend queries. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo proxy hop origin model increment in proxy rewrite and parser-consistency threat model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace downstream and upstream connections, hop-by-hop versus end-to-end fields, termination points, authority, scheme, client address, and failure ownership.
Bound costly domain actions by principal, resource, workflow state, velocity, inventory, and anomaly evidence beyond raw request rate.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Proxy Hop Origin Model · Theory Proxy Hop Origin Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3485 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject ambiguous framing and deprecated Director-style proxy mutation; then remove one trusted intermediary and recompute authority without forwarded fields. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
